query: Abstract Wikipedia 1000 registered users Lists first new type milestone early 2024 Wikimedia

{"query":"Abstract Wikipedia 1000 registered users Lists first new type milestone early 2024 Wikimedia","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1743,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Wikipedia_Signpost/2026-01-15/Special_report","title":"Wikipedia:Wikipedia Signpost/2026-01-15/Special report - Wikipedia","content":"On 15 January, 2026, Wikipedia turns 25.
Sixty million articles in over 300 languages.
Hindi Wikipedia has 160,000 articles for 600 million speakers.
Internet users grew ~18% annually.
For 25 years, we've talked about being "the sum of all human knowledge." We've celebrated our 300+ language editions.
If Wikimedia had grown at the same rate as internet users, we would have 355B page views today instead of 177B.
Early data (2001-2007) is less complete than recent data."},{"url":"https://abstract.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_Wikipedia:Main_page","title":"Abstract Wikipedia","content":"Abstract Wikipedia is a Wikimedia project for everyone to collaboratively create and maintain a collection of language-independent articles, based on functions from Wikifunctions and data from Wikidata, to help Wikimedia projects to expand their article base.
The next Volunteers' Corner will be at 17:30 UTC on 2026-09-07 on Google Meet at meet.google.com/xuy-njxh-rkw.
2026-07-16 Beyond syntactic tables
Our first featured abstract articles, coming soon!"},{"url":"https://diff.wikimedia.org/2026/01/14/wikimedia-foundation-bulletin-2026-issue-1/","title":"Wikimedia Foundation Bulletin 2026 Issue 1 – Diff","content":"Here is a quick overview of highlights from the Wikimedia Foundation since our last issue on December 17.
Join the virtual celebration featuring musical performances, games, and more on January 15 at 16:00 UTC. and The party will be live interpreted into Arabic, Chinese, French, Spanish, and Portuguese.
Apply to join the Northwestern Europe 2026 Hackathon from March 13-14 in Arnhem, the Netherlands.
Wikimedia Research Showcase: The research showcase will return on January 22 at 17:30 UTC with the theme “Celebrating 25 Years of Wikipedia and the Research Behind It”."},{"url":"https://wikimediafoundation.org/","title":"Home – Wikimedia Foundation","content":"Celebrate Wikipedia’s 25th birthday
Mark this milestone by paying tribute to the hundreds of thousands of people that edit, write, and fact check the backbone of knowledge on the internet.
She’s since inspired people to upload over 15,000 new photos.
Over the last two decades, he’s made over 760,000 improvements across Wikimedia projects."},{"url":"https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Wikimedia_Foundation/News_and_Events/en","title":"Wikimedia Foundation/News and Events - Meta-Wiki","content":"This includes the virtual birthday event on January 15, which garnered 10,000 live viewers and 15,000 reactions.
Sustainable use of Wikimedia infrastructure: A valid user-agent string will now be required for automated dumps downloads from the dumps.wikimedia.org website.
Tech News: The latest highlights from Tech News week 26 and 27 include users will now get a notification when they are blocked or unblocked from editing, or if this block changes.
Wikimedia Hackathon: A look at the 2026 edition of Wikimedia Hackathon which brought together 216 participants from 29 countries building, collaborating, and shaping the future together.
Wikimedia Hubs: The Hub Fund will pause funding for new pilots in fiscal year 2026–2027 to align with the work on the Ecosystem of Movement Organizations and the Global Resource Distribution Committee.
Journalism Award: Wikimedia Foundation announced three journalists from Africa as recipients of the Open the Knowledge Journalism Awards, run in partnership with the International Center for Journalists (ICFJ)."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Wikipedia_Signpost/Single/2026-01-15","title":"Wikipedia:Wikipedia Signpost/Single/2026-01-15","content":"January 15, 2026, marks the 25th anniversary of the 2001 inception of Wikipedia. Sixty million articles in over 300 languages. Registrations +10%/yr. Wikimedia ..."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Abstract Wikipedia 1000 registered users Lists first new type milestone early 2024 Wikimedia","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Abstract_Wikipedia/Updates/2024-01-25","title":"Abstract Wikipedia/Updates/2024-01-25","content":"Abstract Wikipedia/Updates/2024-01-25 … improved our code documentation for people getting started with our code. Wikimedia Meta-Wiki Wikimedia","score":0.418341,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://enterprise.wikimedia.com/blog/2024-api-wrap-up","title":"2024 Wikimedia Enterprise API Evolution & Wikipedia Milestones","content":"# 2024 Wikimedia Enterprise API Milestones & Wikipedia’s Evolving Data Landscape. The constant evolution of Wikipedia’s content has never been more evident than in 2024, with English Wikipedia alone seeing *over 31 million* edits. As we wrap up the year, let’s explore how our Enterprise APIs have evolved to help harness this wealth of information more effectively, as well as examine the patterns in Wikipedia’s usage data. * 2024 Wikipedia by the Numbers. ## Living Knowledge: 2024 Wikipedia by the Numbers. These numbers underscore the nature of Wikipedia’s content–precisely the kind of real-time information our APIs help to access and analyze. curl --location 'https://api.enterprise.wikimedia.com/v2/articles/Deaths_in_2024' \\ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \\ --header 'Authorization: Bearer {YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN}' \\ --data '{ \"filters\": [ { \"field\":\"is_part_of.identifier\", \"value\": \"enwiki\" } ] }'. curl --location 'https://api.enterprise.wikimedia.com/v2/articles/2024_United_States_presidential_election' \\ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \\ --header 'Authorization: Bearer {YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN}' \\ --data '{ \"filters\": [ { \"field\":\"is_part_of.identifier\", \"value\": \"enwiki\" } ] }'.","score":0.379241,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Abstract_Wikipedia","title":"Abstract Wikipedia - Meta-Wiki","content":"This project consists of two parts: Abstract Wikipedia and Wikifunctions. **Abstract Wikipedia** is a conceptual extension of Wikidata and the aspirational reification of a more accessible Wikipedia. In Abstract Wikipedia, people can create and maintain Wikipedia articles in a language-independent way. With the help of functions and code, any non-English language Wikipedia would be able to have this language-independent article translated into their respective language. **Wikifunctions** is a new Wikimedia project that allows anyone to create and maintain code. It also provides code that translates the language-independent article from Abstract Wikipedia into the language of a Wikipedia. Once more pieces of this project are in place, this will mean that any wiki – especially small to medium wikis – will be able to dramatically increase the number of articles available in their language. In other words: we will be able to combine the functions from the new wiki, with the data and linguistic-information in Wikidata, in order to generate natural language sentences in any supported languages.","score":0.34733838,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_Wikipedia","title":"Abstract Wikipedia - Wikipedia","content":"# Abstract Wikipedia. *(Learn how and when to remove these messages)* |  | | --- | | This article **relies excessively on references to primary sources**. *Find sources:* \"Abstract Wikipedia\" – news **·** newspapers **·** books **·** scholar **·** JSTOR *(August 2023)* *(Learn how and when to remove this message)* |  |  | | --- | | This article needs to be **updated**. **Abstract Wikipedia** is an in-development project of the Wikimedia Foundation. First conceived in 2020 (with a precursor proposal in 2013), Abstract Wikipedia has been under active development ever since, with the related project of Wikifunctions launched in 2023. As envisioned, Abstract Wikipedia would consist of \"Constructors\" (templates for abstract statements), \"Content\" (the abstract statements themselves), and \"Renderers\" (which would automatically translate abstract statements into natural language). On 7 August 2013, Denny Vrandečić, the co-founder of Wikidata, suggested \"an extension of the template system\" where template calls would expand into content based on the language of the user.","score":0.34680724,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://wikimediafoundation.org/story/abstract-wikipedia","title":"Abstract Wikipedia – Wikimedia Foundation","content":"## Our technology supports the backbone of knowledge on the internet. ## The more people take part, the better the internet’s knowledge gets. Donate to help keep Wikipedia free and accessible. When Wikipedia debuted on the internet in 2001, the first articles were in English, but everyone from founder Jimmy Wales to Wikipedia’s first volunteer editors wanted the website to quickly evolve into a multilingual knowledge source. Abstract Wikipedia will accelerate language equality across Wikipedia, evolving Wikipedias with small article bases into full-fledged knowledge sources that benefit hundreds of millions of people. Through Wikifunctions, volunteer editors will contribute technological functions to translate language-independent articles from Abstract Wikipedia into articles for different language Wikipedias—from Urdu to Igbo, and Swahili to Burmese. The Wikimedia Foundation’s vision is a world where “every single human being can freely share in the sum of all knowledge.” Abstract Wikipedia will help us achieve this ambitious goal. Follow the Wikimedia Foundation on social media to get news and updates on our projects, global community, and advocacy efforts.","score":0.2812506,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":2.56,"request_id":"9d536eaf-c0c3-4348-9f9d-36d7e93f47c7"}
